commit.message:
feat: orchestration verification pipeline — requirement-level coverage, review, and HTML reporting

Adds end-to-end requirement traceability to the orchestration pipeline:
- Coverage enforcement at plan time (require_full_coverage directive)
- Requirement-aware LLM code review (per-change REQ injection)
- Final coverage assertion at all 5 monitor loop exit paths
- HTML report generator with digest/plan/execution/coverage sections
- Coverage summary in email notifications
- 67 integration tests (orchestrate) + 55 integration tests (digest)

Fix CMD+1-9 shortcuts to match sidebar session order (#26)

## Summary
- CMD+1-9 shortcuts now switch sessions in the same order they appear in
the sidebar (named groups alphabetically → ungrouped, destroyed sessions
last within each group)
- Extracted `sidebarOrderSessions()` as a pure, testable function in
`SessionContext`
- Added 9 unit tests covering all ordering scenarios

Closes #4

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run test` — all 1650 tests pass (including 9 new ones)
- [ ] Manual: create sessions in multiple project groups, verify CMD+1-9
matches sidebar order
- [ ] Manual: destroy a session, verify it moves to the end of its group
for shortcuts

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

All pages build as static, consistent design system throughout.

add 5x5 cube support with scramble and 3D preview

moves (floor(5/2)=2), blocking same face+layer and opposite face+layer.

with grid-cols-5.

feat: Optimize four pillars scoring — evidence specificity, modeled validation, DB indexes

Citable: Evidence quality scoring now requires data-level specifics (row_ids,
column_ids, query, or row_ranges) instead of counting any non-empty evidence
dict. Backfilled generic metadata (dataset_id) no longer inflates the score.

Accurate: Modeled finding validation now requires model_type field from a
defined vocabulary and population_scope in the scope dict.

Performant: Migration 29 adds composite indexes on plugin_results_v2
(status, run_id) and (execution_fingerprint, status) for the common
query patterns in report/prune/lineage paths.

commit.message:
Make PRD optional — allow design-only reviews

Users can now skip the PRD step and review designs based on visual
principles alone. When no PRD is provided, the PRD Parser agent is
skipped and remaining agents evaluate using design heuristics instead
of PRD compliance. UI adapts throughout: skip link on PRD step,
conditional agent count, and context-aware copy.

commit.message:
fix: agent modal cannot resize downward

The backdrop used absolute positioning, so getWorkspaceRect() returned
the settings panel height instead of the full viewport. This clamped
maxBottomHeight and prevented the modal from growing downward.

Change backdrop from absolute to fixed so it covers the entire viewport
and resize calculations use the correct available space.

commit.message:
fix: stop infinite polling of /api/settings/agents/skills

Root cause: useTranslation() returned a new `t` function reference on
every render. Since `t` was in the useEffect dependency array that
fetches skills, every parent re-render re-triggered the fetch.

Two fixes:
1. Memoize `t` with useCallback in useTranslation() so the reference
   is stable per locale — prevents this class of bug globally
2. Remove `t` from the skills-fetch useEffect deps (only agent.id
   matters for deciding when to refetch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(graph): fix hover highlight not restoring + remove redundant project legend

- Merge 3 independent highlight useEffects (legend, project, group)
  into a single unified effect with priority-based state computation.
  The old save/restore pattern with separate dirty refs caused stale
  sprite states when effects competed over the same sprites.
- Remove bottom project legend in workspace graph (redundant with
  top LayerControls project filter bar).
- Move project hover highlight to the top project buttons via
  onHoverProject prop on LayerControls.
- Remove LiveIndicator (offline button) from project graph page.

commit.message:
Add Rust implementation for better performance (Step2)

- Add Rust Boids implementation with SoA data structure
- Use Rayon for parallel processing
- Add Dockerfile.rust for Rust build
- Update docker-compose.yml to use Rust version
- Optimize Go version with spatial hash improvements
- Add optimization.md documenting performance analysis

Rust version achieves ~2.5x speedup over optimized Go:
- 100K particles: 28-30fps (vs 12-16fps in Go)
- Stable real-time streaming at 30K particles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(backtester): stop-on-tick guarantee, strategy exit params, DTE-DOW off by default

- Add _stop_clamp() helper: caps realized_pnl at configured stop threshold on all
  loss-exit paths (FRIDAY_CLOSEOUT x2, RISK_STOP_5PCT, PER_TRADE_STOP, DOLLAR_STOP)
- Update tr.pnl_pct from capped realized_pnl on all stop-exit paths so closed_trades
  reflects true final outcome, not pre-cap bar-level value
- Remove dead STRATEGY_PROFIT_TARGETS / STRATEGY_DOLLAR_STOPS / STRATEGY_STOP_LOSS
  dicts (keyed by class_name, looked up by template_id — never matched)
- Remove --profittargets flag; profit_target now always read from strategy CONFIG
- Fix strategy_optimizer.py: remove use_profit_targets kwarg from both run_backtest calls
- Populate all 55 strategy scripts with stop_loss_mult, stop_loss_dollar, profit_target,
  max_contracts per tight-stop expectancy model (SL<=$1000, PT>=2xSL)
- Add scripts/populate_strategy_exits.py batch population script
- --dte-mon/tue/wed/thu/fri now default=None (off); only activate when explicitly set
- Add v46 transition plan v2 and task checklist to docs/plans/

stub ordering (pre-source for log/model, post-source for notification).
